usage: report_clock_timing [-help] [-absolute_compare] [-clock <clock_list>] [-cppr_relative] [-format <column_list>] [-from <from_list>] [-from_clock <from_clock_list>] [-greater_than <lower_limit>] [-latency_greater_than <lower_limit>] [-nworst <worst_entries>] [-tcl_list] [-to <to_list>] [-to_clock <to_clock_list>] -type {skew interclock_skew jitter summary latency cppr_stage_count} [-verbose] [-view <view_list>] [> <filename>] [>> <filename>] [-early  | -late ] [ [-rise  | -fall ] [-launch  | -capture ] | [-histogram  [-histogram_range <interval_size>]] [-logic_level  [-source {clock_root | generated_clock}]] ]

options.name: -help
options.description: Prints out the command usage

options.name: -absolute_compare
options.description: Reports skews with values compared in absolute form

options.name: -capture
options.description: When specified with -type latency, generates a report of capture latency values (-capture)

options.name: -clock
options.description: List of clocks to be reported

options.name: -cppr_relative
options.description: Reports the latencies in skew reports, from the cppr common pin

options.name: -early
options.description: Uses hold skew to generate the report

options.name: -fall
options.description: Active transition at sequential devices clock pins on falling edge

options.name: -format
options.description: Columns to display for path reports, and the order in which they should appear

options.name: -from
options.description: List of sequential device clock pins or ports to consider as from-pins in the generated report

options.name: -from_clock
options.description: List of from-clocks to be used in generating the current interclock skew report

options.name: -greater_than
options.description: Reports skews with values greater than the lower_limit value

options.name: -histogram
options.description: Generates a textual histogram of the latency report

options.name: -histogram_range
options.description: Generates the histogram report at the specified intervals.

options.name: -late
options.description: Uses setup skew to generate the report

options.name: -latency_greater_than
options.description: When specified with type (skew | interclock_skew | cppr_stage_count), the reports are filtered for latency (in case of skew/interclock_skew) greater than provided value or depth (in case of cppr_stage_count) greater than provided value

options.name: -launch
options.description: When specified with -type latency, generates a report of launch latency values (-launch)

options.name: -logic_level
options.description: Reports the levels (the stage count of gates) between the clock root, or generated clock root, and the capturing register

options.name: -nworst
options.description: Number of entries to be reported per clock domain

options.name: -rise
options.description: Active transition at sequential devices clock pins on rising edge

options.name: -source
options.description: Reports the latency from either the master clock, or the generated clock that was derived from it

options.name: -tcl_list
options.description: Produces the report in Tcl list format instead of a tabular format

options.name: -to
options.description: List of sequential device clock pins or ports to consider as to-pins in the generated report

options.name: -to_clock
options.description: List of to-clocks to be used in generating the current interclock skew report

options.name: -type
options.description: {skew interclock_skew jitter summary latency cppr_stage_count} Type of report to generate

options.name: -verbose
options.description: Generates a report that contains the full clock path from the clock source to the sink pin

options.name: -view
options.description: Generates a report for the specified analysis view only (string, optional) > <filename> # Writes the report to the specified file name (redirect_operator, optional) >> <filename> # Writes the report to the specified file name
